claim: Independent verification confirms YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements scoped evidence separation where WebFetch/WebSearch results are marked as external research and cannot satisfy existing-project fact requirements without project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classification logic and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() validation that blocks external-only evidence from satisfying project facts, integrated into PRD readiness check
evidence.why: Core implementation proves scope separation is enforced

evidence.summary: Agent prompt rules requiring WebFetch/WebSearch records as scope=external, runtime scope enforcement, and provider configuration for research profiles
evidence.why: Contract enforcement and provider configuration prove external research is marked as external scope

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states external evidence cannot prove target project implementation facts and defines three evidence agent protocols
evidence.why: Product specification confirms external evidence is background research only

evidence.summary: Test coverage for scope enforcement (EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED) and research profile configuration
evidence.why: Test suite validates scope declaration requirements and WebFetch/WebSearch capability

risks: Agent providers could ignore scope declaration rules and treat external research as project facts
WebFetch/WebSearch tools might be unavailable despite research profile configuration
Boundary audit could have false positives/negatives in mutation detection

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is consistent, well-sourced, and complete. No assumptions are presented as facts. All evidence paths reference existing project files. Both agents independently identified the same risks and explicitly listed them in the risks array rather than promoting them to facts. The evidence sufficiently supports PRD readiness for the scoped evidence separation requirement.
